Package.name: CFE_TA
Package.shortDescription: NASA Core Flight System Task Affinity Services

Entry.name: FileName
Entry.shortDescription: ASCII text string containing full path and filename of file in which Application data is to be dumped

Entry.name: TaskName
Entry.shortDescription: ASCII text string containing the task name

Entry.name: TaskName
Entry.shortDescription: ASCII text string containing the task name

Entry.name: StartupCounter
Entry.shortDescription: Count of successful startup affinity configs

Entry.name: StartupErrorCounter
Entry.shortDescription: Count of invalid startup affinity configs

ContainerDataType.name: NoopCmd
ContainerDataType.LongDescription:
\cfetacmd Task Affinity No-Op
\par Description This command performs no other function than to increment the command execution counter. The command may be used to verify general aliveness of the Task Affinity Services task.
\par Command Structure
\par Command Verification Successful execution of this command may be verified with the following telemetry: - \b \c \TA_CMDPC - command execution counter will increment - The #CFE_TA_NOOP_INF_EID informational event message will be generated
\par Error Conditions There are no error conditions for this command. If the Task Affinity Services receives the command, the event is sent (although it may be filtered by EVS) and the counter is incremented unconditionally.
\par Criticality None

ContainerDataType.name: ResetCountersCmd
ContainerDataType.LongDescription:
\cfetacmd Task Affinity Reset Counters
\par Description This command resets the following counters within the Task Affinity Services housekeeping telemetry: - Command Execution Counter (\TA_CMDPC) - Command Error Counter (\TA_CMDEC)
\par Command Structure
\par Command Verification Successful execution of this command may be verified with the following telemetry: - \b \c \TA_CMDPC - command execution counter will be reset to 0 - The #CFE_TA_RESET_COUNTERS_INF_EID debug event message will be generated
\par Error Conditions There are no error conditions for this command. If the Task Affinity Services receives the command, the event is sent (although it may be filtered by EVS) and the counters are reset unconditionally.
\par Criticality This command is not inherently dangerous. However, it is possible for ground systems and on-board safing procedures to be designed such that they react to changes in the counter values that are reset by this command.

ContainerDataType.name: QueryAllTasksCmd
ContainerDataType.LongDescription:
\cfetacmd Query All Tasks
\par Description This command will cause Task Affinity Services to query the affinity settings for all tasks in the system and write the results to the specified file.
\par Command Structure
\par Command Verification Successful execution of this command may be verified with the following telemetry: - \b \c \TA_CMDPC - command execution counter will increment - The #CFE_TA_TASKINFO_EID informational event message will be generated
\par Error Conditions This command may fail for the following reason(s): - Error occurred during write operation to file. Possible causes might be insufficient space in the file system or the filename or file path is improperly specified.
Evidence of failure may be found in the following telemetry: - \b \c \TA_CMDEC - command error counter will increment - Command specific error event message is issued for all error cases
\par Criticality This command is not inherently dangerous. It will create a new file in the file system and could, if performed repeatedly without sufficient file management by the operator, fill the file system.

ContainerDataType.name: SetTaskAffinityCmd
ContainerDataType.LongDescription:
\cfetacmd Set Task Affinity
\par Description This command will set the processor affinity for the specified task to the specified affinity mask value.
\par Command Structure
\par Command Verification Successful execution of this command may be verified with the following telemetry: - \b \c \TA_CMDPC - command execution counter will increment - The #CFE_TA_SET_TASK_AFFINITY_CMD_INF_EID informational event message will be generated
\par Error Conditions This command may fail for the following reason(s): - The specified task name was not found in the system. - The affinity mask value is invalid for the target platform. - An OS error occurred while attempting to set the affinity.
Evidence of failure may be found in the following telemetry: - \b \c \TA_CMDEC - command error counter will increment - Command specific error event message is issued for all error cases
\par Criticality This command can affect system performance and task scheduling behavior. Improper use could result in tasks not executing as expected.

ContainerDataType.name: GetTaskAffinityCmd
ContainerDataType.LongDescription:
\cfetacmd Get Task Affinity
\par Description This command will query and report the current processor affinity setting for the specified task via an event message.
\par Command Structure
\par Command Verification Successful execution of this command may be verified with the following telemetry: - \b \c \TA_CMDPC - command execution counter will increment - The #CFE_TA_GET_TASK_AFFINITY_CMD_INF_EID informational event message will be generated containing the current affinity mask value
\par Error Conditions This command may fail for the following reason(s): - The specified task name was not found in the system. - An OS error occurred while attempting to query the affinity.
Evidence of failure may be found in the following telemetry: - \b \c \TA_CMDEC - command error counter will increment - Command specific error event message is issued for all error cases
\par Criticality This is an informational command with no side effects.
